speaker: TALBOT
text: Saint George, and victory! Fight, soldiers, fight!

speaker: TALBOT
text: The Regent hath with Talbot broke his word

speaker: TALBOT
text: And left us to the rage of France his sword.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Where is John Talbot?—Pause, and take thy breath;

speaker: TALBOT
text: I gave thee life and rescued thee from death.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: O, twice my father, twice am I thy son!

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: The life thou gav’st me first was lost and done

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Till with thy warlike sword, despite of fate,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: To my determined time thou gav’st new date.

speaker: TALBOT
text: When from the Dauphin’s crest thy sword struck fire,

speaker: TALBOT
text: It warmed thy father’s heart with proud desire

speaker: TALBOT
text: Of bold-faced victory. Then leaden age,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Quickened with youthful spleen and warlike rage,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Beat down Alanson, Orleance, Burgundy,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And from the pride of Gallia rescued thee.

speaker: TALBOT
text: The ireful Bastard Orleance, that drew blood

speaker: TALBOT
text: From thee, my boy, and had the maidenhood

speaker: TALBOT
text: Of thy first fight, I soon encounterèd,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And, interchanging blows, I quickly shed

speaker: TALBOT
text: Some of his bastard blood, and in disgrace

speaker: TALBOT
text: Bespoke him thus: “Contaminated, base,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And misbegotten blood I spill of thine,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Mean and right poor, for that pure blood of mine

speaker: TALBOT
text: Which thou didst force from Talbot, my brave boy.”

speaker: TALBOT
text: Here, purposing the Bastard to destroy,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Came in strong rescue. Speak, thy father’s care:

speaker: TALBOT
text: Art thou not weary, John? How dost thou fare?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Wilt thou yet leave the battle, boy, and fly,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Now thou art sealed the son of chivalry?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Fly, to revenge my death when I am dead;

speaker: TALBOT
text: The help of one stands me in little stead.

speaker: TALBOT
text: O, too much folly is it, well I wot,

speaker: TALBOT
text: To hazard all our lives in one small boat.

speaker: TALBOT
text: If I today die not with Frenchmen’s rage,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Tomorrow I shall die with mickle age.

speaker: TALBOT
text: By me they nothing gain, and, if I stay,

speaker: TALBOT
text: ’Tis but the short’ning of my life one day.

speaker: TALBOT
text: In thee thy mother dies, our household’s name,

speaker: TALBOT
text: My death’s revenge, thy youth, and England’s fame.

speaker: TALBOT
text: All these and more we hazard by thy stay;

speaker: TALBOT
text: All these are saved if thou wilt fly away.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: The sword of Orleance hath not made me smart;

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: These words of yours draw lifeblood from my heart.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: On that advantage, bought with such a shame,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: To save a paltry life and slay bright fame,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Before young Talbot from old Talbot fly,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: The coward horse that bears me fall and die!

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: And like me to the peasant boys of France,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: To be shame’s scorn and subject of mischance!

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Surely, by all the glory you have won,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: An if I fly, I am not Talbot’s son.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: Then talk no more of flight, it is no boot;

speaker:
JOHN
               
              TALBOT
text: If son to Talbot, die at Talbot’s foot.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Then follow thou thy desp’rate sire of Crete,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Thou Icarus; thy life to me is sweet.

speaker: TALBOT
text: If thou wilt fight, fight by thy father’s side,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And commendable proved, let’s die in pride.
